framing: Freelance content strategist applying to vague Head of Content role at Series B fintech (culture-first JD)

jd.title: Head of Content
jd.style_note: Vague/culture-first: minimal requirements, personality-focused, no specific tools or years listed

requirements.text: You've built content programs that people actually want to read — not just SEO filler
You think in systems, not just individual pieces of content
You can write, edit, and give feedback that makes writers better
You've managed a small team or a roster of freelancers
You care about financial literacy and making money less confusing for everyone

responsibilities: Own Monzo's content strategy across blog, in-app messaging, email, and social
Build a content team (currently 1 writer, growing to 4)
Set the editorial voice and quality bar for everything Monzo publishes
Work with product, marketing, and CRM teams to align content with business goals
Figure out how to measure whether content is actually working

expected_output.summary: Freelance content strategist with 7 years of experience across fintech, SaaS, and media — 4 years freelancing for clients including Starling Bank, Wise, and GoCardless, and 3 years in-house at The Guardian's branded content studio. Built editorial systems and content programs that drove measurable engagement, not just traffic. Portfolio includes long-form guides, product communications, and email sequences. Managed teams of writers and freelancer rosters up to 8.

experience.title: Freelance Content Strategist
bullets: Built content programs for 12 fintech and SaaS clients including Starling Bank, Wise, GoCardless, and Tide
Created Starling Bank's financial literacy blog series (24 articles) — became their highest-traffic organic content, averaging 45K monthly visits
Designed editorial guidelines and content governance frameworks for 4 scaling startups
Managed roster of 6-8 freelance writers per project, providing briefs, edits, and quality feedback
Developed content measurement frameworks linking blog engagement to product sign-up attribution

experience.title: Senior Content Producer — Branded Content
bullets: Produced branded content campaigns for financial services clients (Barclays, HSBC, Nationwide)
Managed team of 3 writers and 2 designers creating long-form editorial content
Led Guardian Labs' fintech content vertical, producing 60+ sponsored articles over 3 years
Won The Drum Awards for Content (2019) for Nationwide financial wellbeing campaign

education: MA Magazine Journalism — City St George's, University of London (2016-2017)
BA English Literature — University of Leeds (2013-2016)

jd_requirements: content programs people want to read not SEO filler
think in systems not individual pieces
write edit feedback makes writers better
deliberate_flaws: Freelancer — no permanent in-house experience at a tech company (Guardian Labs is media, not tech)
Has worked for Monzo competitor Starling — potential conflict or bias perception
No experience with in-app messaging or CRM content (all blog/editorial focused)
Content measurement is self-described frameworks, not proven analytics tooling (no Amplitude, Mixpanel, etc.)
Vague JD makes it hard to match — candidate must infer what 'thinking in systems' means
No social media content experience mentioned
Team management was project-based freelancer rosters, not permanent employees

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
